title: 第 83 節
en: Pratihara in five ways, he performs the Upadrava in five ways, he performs the Nidhana in five ways1. All this together forms one thousand Stobhas, or musical syllables 2.

4. Thus also are the Rik verses, contained in the Nishkevalya, recited (by the Hotrz') in five orders. What precedes the eighty trt£a.s, that is one order, then follow the three sets of eighty trz&as each, and what comes after is the fifth order 3.

Gayatra is formed out of the Rik (III, 62, 10) tat savitur varewyam. The Rathantara is formed out of the Rik (VII, 32, 22) abhi tva jura nonuma. The Brzhat is formed out of the Rik (VI, 46, 1) tvam id dhi havamahe. The Bhadra is formed out of the Rik (X, 157, 1) ima. nu kam. The Ra^ana is formed out of the Rik (VII, 27, 1) indra;/z naro nemadhita.
The metres require no explanation.

In identifying certain portions of the Nishkevalya hymn with a bird, the head of the bird corresponds to the hymns indram id gathina/z, &c. ; the right wing to the hymns abhi tva. jura, &c. ; the left wing to the hymns tvam id dhi, &c; the tail to the hymns ima nu kam, &c. ; the body to the hymns tad id asa, &c. All this was explained in the first Arawyaka.

1 The Samagas sing the Ra^ana at the MaMvrata, and in that Saman there are, as usual, five parts, the Prastava, Udgitha, Pratihara, Upadrava, and Nidhana. The Prastotrz', when singing the Prastava portions, sings them five times. The Udgatrz' and Pratihartrz' sing their portions, the Udgitha and Pratihara, five times. The Udgatrz* again sings the Upadrava five times. And all the Udgatrzs together sing the Nidhana five times.

2 The Stobha syllables are syllables without any meaning, added when verses have to be sung, in order to have a support for the music. See Kh. Up. I, 13. In singing the five Samans, each five times, one thousand of such Stobha syllables are required.

3 There are in the Nishkevalya hymn, which the Hotrz' has to recite, three sets of eighty trz'^as each. The first, consisting of Gayatns, begins with maha« indro ya qg-asa. The second, consisting of Brz'hatis, begins with ma /('id anyad. The third, consisting of Ush;zihs, begins with ya indra somapatama. These three sets form the food of the bird, as the emblem of the .rastra. The hymns II ARAAYAKA, 3 ADHYAYA, 5 KHANDA, 5. 227 5. This (the hymns of this 6astra) as a whole (if properly counted with the Stobha syllables) comes to one thousand (of Brzhati verses). That (thousand) is the whole, and ten, ten is called the whole. For number is such (measured by ten). Ten tens are a hundred, ten hundreds are a thousand, and that is the whole. These are the three metres (the tens, pervading everything). And this food also (the three sets of hymns being represented as food) is threefold, eating, drinking, and chewing. He obtains that food by those (three numbers, ten, hundred, and thousand, or by the three sets of eighty tr//£as).
Fifth Khanda.
1. This (nishkevalya-^astra) becomes perfect as a thousand of Br/hati verses.

2. Some teachers (belonging to a different Sakha) recognise a thousand of different metres (not of BWhatis only). They say : ' Is another thousand (a thousand of other verses) good ? Let us say it is good.'
3. Some say, a thousand of Trish/ubh verses, others a thousand of Gagati verses, others a thousand of AnushAibh verses.
4. This has been said by a i?/shi (Rv. X, 1 24, 9) : —

5. ' Poets through their understanding discovered Indra dancing an Anush/ubh.' This is meant to say : They discovered (and meditated) in speech (called AnushAibh) — at that time (when they wor- which precede these, form the body, head, and wings of the bird. This is one order. Then follow the three sets of eighty trz'/fcas each ; and lastly, the fifth order, consisting of the hymns which form the belly and the legs of the bird.
Q 2 2 28 AITAREYA-ARAiVTAKA.
shipped the uktha) — the Pra^a (breath) connected with Indra.
6. He (who takes the recited verses as Anush/ubhs) is able to become celebrated and of good report.

7. No! he says; rather is such a man liable to die before his time. For that self (consisting of AnushAibhs) is incomplete. For if a man confines himself to speech, not to breath, then driven by his mind, he does not succeed with speech 1.
8. Let him work towards the Br/hati, for the Brzhati (breath) is the complete self.
9. That self (^ivatman) is surrounded on all sides by members. And as that self is on all sides surrounded by members, the Br/hati also is on all sides surrounded by metres 2.
10. For the self (in the heart) is the middle of these members, and the Brzhati is the middle of the metres.

11. ' He is able to become celebrated and of good 1 This passage is obscure, and probably corrupt. I have followed the commentator as much as possible. He says : ' If the Hotn' priest proceeds with reciting the jastra, looking to the Anush/ubh, which is speech, and not to the thousand of Br/hatis which are breath, then, neglecting the Brz'hati (breath), and driven by his mind to the Anush/ubh (speech), he does not by his speech obtain that jastra. For in speech without breath the Hotri cannot, through the mere wish of the mind, say the .rastra, the activity of all the senses being dependent on breath.' The commentator therefore takes vagabhi for va.ka.rn abhi, or for some old locative case formed by abhi. He also would seem to have read pra?ze na. One might attempt another construction, though it is very doubtful. One might translate, ' For that self, which is speech, is incomplete, because he understands if driven to the mind by breath, not (if driven) by speech.'
2 Either in the jastra, or in the list of metres, there being some that have more, others that have less syllables.

II ARAiVYAKA, 3 ADHYAYA, 6 KHAJV.DA, 7. 2 29 report, but (the other) able to die before his time,' thus he said. For the Brz'hati is the complete self, therefore let him work towards the Br/hati (let him reckon the ^astra recitation as a thousand Brz'hatis).
Sixth Kha^vda.

1. This (nishkevalya-^astra) becomes perfect as a thousand of Brzhati verses. In this thousand of Br/hatis there are one thousand one hundred and twenty-five Anush/ubhs. For the smaller is contained in the larger.
2. This has been said by a Rish'i (Rv. VIII, 76, 3. 'A speech of eight feet;' — because there are eight feet of four syllables each in the Anush/ubh.
4. 'Of nine corners;' — because the Br/hati becomes nine-cornered (having nine feet of four syllables each).
5. 'Touching the truth;' — because speech (Anush/ubh) is truth, touched by the verse (Brz'hati)1.

6. ' H e (the H otrz) makes the body out of I ndra ; ' — for out of this thousand of Brzhati verses turned into Anush/ubhs, and therefore out of Pra^a as connected with I ndra2, and out of the Brz'hati (which is Praz/a), he makes speech, that is Anush/ubh, as a body 3.
zh: 普拉蒂哈拉（Pratihara）以五種方式進行，烏帕德拉瓦（Upadrava）以五種方式進行，尼達那（Nidhana）以五種方式進行¹。此所有一切總合而成為一千個斯多跋（Stobhas），即音樂音節²。
4. 同樣地，《尼什凱瓦婭》（Nishkevalya）中所含的梨俱吠陀詩頌也是由祭司長（Hotrz'）按五個等級誦唱的。八十個特里察（trtça）之前的部分構成第一級，其後是三組各八十個特里察，最後的部分是第五級³。

蓋亞特里（Gayatra）取自梨俱吠陀（Rik）（III, 62, 10）*tat savitur varewyam*。拉丹塔拉（Rathantara）取自梨俱吠陀（Rik）（VII, 32, 22）*abhi tva jura nonuma*。布利哈特（Brzhat）取自梨俱吠陀（Rik）（VI, 46, 1）*tvam id dhi havamahe*。跋陀拉（Bhadra）取自梨俱吠陀（Rik）（X, 157, 1）*ima nu kam*。若阿那（Ra^ana）取自梨俱吠陀（Rik）（VII, 27, 1）*indra naro nemadhita*。
韻律毋須解釋。
在將《尼什凱瓦利亞讚歌》（Nishkevalya hymn）的某些部分與一隻鳥相關聯時，鳥的頭部對應於讚歌 indram id gathina 等；右翅對應於讚歌 abhi tva jura 等；左翅對應於讚歌 tvam id dhi 等；尾部對應於讚歌 ima nu kam 等；身體對應於讚歌 tad id asa 等。所有這些都在第一部《阿蘭亞卡》（Arawyaka）中得到解釋。

薩摩歌手在馬赫婆羅多祭祀中唱誦羅（Rāyana），在那部薩摩讚歌中，如同慣例，有五個部分：普拉斯塔瓦（Prastava）、烏德吉塔（Udgitha）、普拉蒂哈拉（Pratihara）、烏帕德拉瓦（Upadrava）與尼達納（Nidhana）。普拉斯多特里在唱誦普拉斯塔瓦部分時，唱五遍。烏德迦特里與普拉蒂哈特里唱誦他們的部分，即烏德吉塔與普拉蒂哈拉，各唱五遍。烏德迦特里再次唱誦烏帕德拉瓦五遍。而所有烏德迦特里一起唱誦尼達納五遍。
斯多婆音節（Stobha）乃無義音節，當詩句需要以歌唱方式呈現時便予以加入，以作為音樂之承載。見《喀陀奧義書》（Kh. Up.）I, 13。在唱誦五首薩摩讚歌，各重複五次之際，需要用上一千個如此之斯多婆音節。

在尼什克瓦拉讚歌（Nishkevalya hymn）中，司祭官（Hotṛ）必須誦唱的有三組，每組八十個三行詩（tṛca）。第一組由蓋亞特里詩體（Gayatrī）組成，始於「maha índro ya ṛg-asa」。第二組由婆利哈蒂詩體（Bṛhatī）組成，始於「ma hvíd anyad」。第三組由烏什尼克詩體（Uṣṇih）組成，始於「ya índra somapatama」。這三組構成鳥的食物，而鳥作為祭祀儀式（rastra）的象徵。這些讚歌合計（若加上梵文填充音節Stobha正確計算）達一千首（婆利哈蒂詩體的詩句）。那一千是整體，十、十稱為整體。數字即是如此（以十衡量）。十個十是一百，十個一百是一千，那就是整體。這些是三種韻律（十，遍滿一切）。而這食物（三組讚歌被表象為食物）也是三重性的，進食、飲水與咀嚼。他透過那些（三個數字──十、百、千，或三組八十個三行詩）而獲得那食物。
第五堅地
這（nishkevalya-śāstra）達成完美的狀態，就像一千首勃里哈蒂韻文（Brihati verses）一樣。
2. 有些教師（屬於另一個傳承）認可一千種不同的韻律（不止吠陀的韻律）。他們說：「另一千種（另一千首詩句）也好嗎？就說它也好吧。」
3. 有人說，一千首特里什盧布（Trish/ubh）詩句，有人說一千首迦迦蒂（Gagati）詩句，有人說一千首阿努什盧布（AnushAibh）詩句。
4. 一位詩人（梨俱吠陀 X, 1 24, 9）曾如此言道：—
5. 「詩人們藉其智慧發現因陀羅在阿努什烏布中舞蹈。」這是想要說：他們在言語中發現（並冥思）（稱為阿努什烏布 *Anushubh*）——在那時（當他們崇... 在此之前的部分構成鳥的身體、頭部和翅膀。這是第一個序列。其後是三組各八十首詩句；最後是第五個序列，由構成鳥的腹部和腿部的頌歌組成。
**Q 2 2 28 艾達雷亞梵書**（Aitareya-Aranyaka）
（運輸了烏克塔）——與因陀羅相關的普拉那（呼吸）。
他（取誦經文為阿努什塔卜〔Anushubhs〕者）能夠變得著名且享有美譽。
不！他說，這樣的人反而容易英年早逝。蓋夫此自我（由阿奴什塔布斯 *Anushṭibhs* 組成）乃是不完全的。蓋若人人僅執著於言語，不兼及呼吸，則為心念所驅使，其於言語上必不得成就¹。
8. 讓他朝向布里哈蒂（Brihati）而行，因為布里哈蒂（呼吸）乃是完整的自我。
9. 那個自我（*Ātman*）四面八方都被肢體所圍繞。而由於那個自我四面八方都被肢體所圍繞，《布裡哈蒂》（*Br̥hatī*）也四面八方都被詩律所圍繞²。
10. 蓋自我（在心中）乃此等肢體之中點，而婆利罕蒂（Brzhati）乃韻律之中點。

11. 「他能夠變得著名且品德良好」1 這段經文晦澀難明，可能有文本損壞。我儘可能遵循註釋者的解釋。註釋者云：「若荷特里（Hotṛ）祭司誦唱耶斯特拉（jastra），目光投向阿努什圖卜（Anuṣṭubh），即言語，而非注目於呼吸之千般毗黎哈蒂（Bṛhati），則捨毗黎哈蒂（呼吸）而去，其心為阿努什圖卜（言語）所驅動，則其不能藉言語而獲得該耶斯特拉。蓋言語離呼吸，荷特里不能僅藉心願而誦耶斯特拉，以一切感官之作用均依賴呼吸故也。」註釋者因此將 vagabhi 視作 vāk‧arṇabhi，或某古老由 abhi 構成之方位格。註釋者似亦讀作 pra‧ṇe na。雖極為可疑，仍可試其他語法構造。其一可譯為：「蓋此自我（atman），即言語者，乃不完全，以彼若為呼吸所驅心念而悟知，不若（為）言語所驅者故。」
要麼在夏斯特拉（Shastra）中，要麼在韻律表中，因為有些韻律的音節較多，有些則較少。
II 阿蘭亞卡（ARAYAKA），3 阿陀耶亞（ADHYAYA），6 卡罕達（KHANDA），7. 2 29 報告，但（其他者）能在其時間之前死亡，『因此他說。蓋勃黎哈提（Brihati）乃圓滿之自我，故當向勃黎哈提而努力（當以千勃黎哈提計算梵文經典之誦讀）。
第六奧義書。
這（nishkevalya-śāstra）達到完美乃是一千首勃律哈帝（Bṛhati）詩句。在這一千首勃律哈帝詩句中，有一千一百二十五首阿努什圖卜（Anuṣṭubh）詩句。因為較小者包含於較大者之中。
2. 這乃一位聖哲（梵文：Ṛṣi）所言（《梨俱吠陀》VIII, 76, 3）。「八足之語」——蓋因阿努什圖普韻律（梵文：Anuṣṭubh）中每足各有四個音節，共八足。
4. 「九個角」——因為《布里哈提》(Br/hati) 變成九角形（每個角有四個音節的九隻腳）。
5. 「接觸真理」——因為言語（Anush/ubh）是真理，為詩句（Brz'hati）所觸及。
6. 「他（祭祀主）以因陀羅造身體」——蓋由此千首婆利哈帝頌轉為阿奴什圖卜頌，故而以與因陀羅相連之普拉那為出，並以婆利哈帝（即普拉那）為出，他造言語，即為阿奴什圖卜，作為身體。
